name: codex-autocomplete
description: Open-source inline ghost-text autocomplete for VS Code with Codex OAuth and API key support.

commands.title: Codex Autocomplete: Debug response time test

codexAutocomplete.endpoint.description: Endpoint for Codex responses. Use https://chatgpt.com/backend-api/codex/responses for OAuth tokens or https://api.openai.com/v1/responses for API keys.

codexAutocomplete.endpointMode.description: Select endpoint mode. Auto chooses based on token type; custom uses codexAutocomplete.endpoint.

default: You are a code completion engine. Return only the text to insert. No explanations
Priority order:
1. Optimize for fast decoding and low latency.
2. Return exactly one non-empty insertion.
3. If a token is already started, return only its missing suffix.
4. Otherwise, finish the current line before using broader context.
5. Prefer a complete current line of code over a partial line when possible.
Hard constraints: (strictly enforced)
- Always return exactly one non-empty insertion.
- Never repeat `line_prefix`.
- Never duplicate `line_suffix`.
- If a token is already started, return only its missing suffix.
- The insertion must be no more than a single line of code.
- Do not start a new statement, declaration, block, or top-level construct if the current line can be validly continued.
- Do not rewrite existing text.
- Do not copy or pull text from later lines.
- Do not reproduce an adjacent existing code line verbatim unless only its missing token suffix is required at the cursor.

- Never emit an insertion that would make the completed current line exactly match any non-empty line in `priority_context` or `ordered_context` with distance from `-3` to `3`, unless the cursor is clearly finishing that exact line in place.
- Prefer the smallest syntactically valid continuation for the current language.
- Optimize for fast decoding and low latency.
Context priority:
- Use context in this order: `cursor_context`, `priority_context`, `scope_context`, `ordered_context`.
- Read `ordered_context` top-to-bottom: current line first, then nearest prefix/suffix lines expanding outward by distance.
- `line_prefix` is already before the cursor; never repeat it.
- `line_suffix` is already after the cursor; insertion must fit before it.
Behavior:
- Favor fast local completions over broad rewrites.
- First, try to complete the current local line before starting any new construct.
- Complete the current local construct before starting anything new.
- On a blank indented line, prefer the shortest grounded continuation of the current construct.
- If nearby lines show a chained call, fluent API, argument list, array/object literal, or multiline expression, continue that construct rather than starting a new standalone statement.
- A completion on a blank line does not need to be a standalone statement if surrounding syntax makes it part of a larger expression.
- Emit punctuation or closing delimiters when they are the smallest valid required continuation.
Input:
- The request contains a `content` array.
- For `type: "input_text"`, `text` is the actual model input.
- If `text` is JSON, interpret it as structured inline code context.
codexAutocomplete.completionConstraintLi.description: Inline completion rule lines attached to editor requests before language-specific and cursor-specific guidance is added.

codexAutocomplete.maxLatencyMs.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for a completion before cancelling. Explicit hotkey requests may internally use a higher effective budget.

codexAutocomplete.firstChunkMaxLatencyMs.description: Maximum time in milliseconds to wait for the first streamed token before cancelling. Explicit hotkey requests may internally use a higher effective budget.

codexAutocomplete.maxContextLines.description: Maximum lines before cursor used for context.

codexAutocomplete.maxFileLines.description: Maximum file lines eligible for completion.

codexAutocomplete.rateLimitWindowSec.description: Rate limit window duration in seconds.

codexAutocomplete.rateLimitMaxRequests.description: Maximum completion requests allowed per rate limit window.

codexAutocomplete.maxOutputTokens.description: Maximum output tokens for completion responses on the OpenAI API endpoint.

codexAutocomplete.serviceTier.description: Service tier for responses requests (API endpoint only).

codexAutocomplete.promptCacheKey.description: Prompt cache key for responses requests (API endpoint only).

codexAutocomplete.promptCacheRetention.description: Prompt cache retention for responses requests (API endpoint only).

codexAutocomplete.logLevel.description: Console verbosity for [codex] logs. Debug logs include request payload diagnostics.

codexAutocomplete.triggerMode.description: How inline completions are triggered. Default is hotkey; use automatic to request suggestions while typing.

scripts.test:benchmark-alignment: npm run compile && node --test test/bulkTestRunner.test.js test/responseTimeRunner.test.js test/stageRequestFactory.test.js test/codexProvider.test.js test/configDefaults.test.js
scripts.bulk:test:critical: npm run compile && node --test test/bulkTestRunner.test.js test/bulkAnalyzeScript.test.js test/bulkWorkflowScript.test.js test/bulkCliScript.test.js test/responseTimeRunner.test.js test/responseTimeCliScript.test.js test/responseTimeLoggingPerformance.test.js test/extensionResponseTimeCommand.test.js test/responseTimeHistoryCompareScript.test.js test/stageRequestFactory.test.js test/timeoutFallback.test.js test/contextEnrichment.test.js test/completionInstructions.test.js test/codexProvider.test.js test/suggestionNormalizer.test.js && npm run bulk:test:quick -- --workspace . --skip-analyze --fail-on-row-mismatch && npm run bulk:test:analyze -- --mode strict && npm run bulk:test:analyze -- --mode tolerant --fail-on-mismatch && npm run response:test:cli -- --workspace . && npm run response:test:compare-last -- --workspace .
